untranslatable_reason: This signal describes an options strategy (calendar spread: short call + long call with different expirations) that cannot be expressed using equity price/technical indicators alone. The core logic requires: (1) option Greeks (theta decay, time value premium) which are not in the allowed indicator set, (2) strike price relationships and expirations which are not price-based inputs, and (3) explicit time decay capture mechanics over a specific holding period. A backtest engine working with equity price action cannot directly model options premium decay and calendar spread P&L.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_112 is a calendar spread strategy from option theory literature. The signal requires option-specific parameters (short_term_call_duration, long_term_call_duration, strike_price_relationship, time_decay_capture_period) that have no equivalent in price-action technical analysis. This strategy is fundamentally untranslatable to a rules engine limited to OHLCV and standard technical indicators.
